steps.title: Identify the horizontal asymptote
steps.content: For an exponential function of the form $f(x)=ab^x+c$, the graph has horizontal asymptote $y=c$. In the picture, the curve levels off near $y=-7$ as it goes left. So $c\approx-7$, not $0$.

steps.title: Compare the graph to the x-axis
steps.content: The question asks how many times $f(x)=0$, which means how many times the graph reaches the $x$-axis. The graph shown stays below the horizontal line $y=-7$, so it is also below the $x$-axis.

steps.title: Count the intersections
steps.content: Since the graph never reaches the $x$-axis, there are zero values of $x$ for which $f(x)=0$. Choice D is correct.
